---
title: "Breakout Strategy Backtest"
subtitle: "FINTECH 533 Project"
---

```{=html}
<script src="https://cdn.plot.ly/plotly-3.4.0.min.js"></script>
<div class="asset-switcher">
  <div>
    <div class="metric-label">Asset View</div>
    <h3 id="active-asset-heading">Loading...</h3>
    <p class="switcher-copy">Use this selector to swap the webpage between assets that were generated in the latest backtest run.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="asset-switcher-control">
    <label for="asset-select">Choose asset</label>
    <select id="asset-select"></select>
    <div id="asset-switcher-status" class="metric-footnote">Loading asset dashboard...</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

This page documents a rules-based breakout strategy built for FINTECH 533. The analysis is designed to be reproducible from the project files, with a walk-forward backtest, downloadable trade blotter, and plain-English explanations of the exact breakout logic.

## Strategy Logic

The strategy looks for assets that are breaking above their recent trading range and then holds those positions only if the move keeps working. Each day, the code checks whether the latest close finished above the highest high from the prior lookback window. If that breakout is confirmed, the strategy buys at the next session's open, sets a volatility-scaled stop-loss and profit target using Average True Range (ATR), and exits on the first event that occurs: profit target hit, stop-loss hit, or a timeout after a fixed number of trading days.

## Asset Selection

The strategy screens a small universe of liquid assets and lets the page switch between the resulting backtests. The table below shows the most recent screening results, and the paragraph underneath updates when you choose a different asset above.

```{=html}
<div id="screening-table"></div>
<p id="asset-selection-text"></p>
```

## Breakout Definition

The breakout detection function is `detect_breakouts()` in `breakout_project.py`. In plain English, it flags a breakout only when today's closing price finishes above the highest high from the previous `N` sessions, where `N` is the Donchian-style lookback window. That prior high is shifted back one bar so the function never uses today's own high to define today's threshold. The same function also computes ATR so the trade management logic can place exits relative to current volatility instead of using arbitrary dollar cutoffs.

All strategy parameters are exposed as named constants near the top of `breakout_project.py`. The current project setup uses:

- Lookback candidates: `20`, `40`, and `55` days
- ATR window: `14` days
- Breakout buffer: `0.00%`
- Stop-loss candidates: `1.5x` or `2.0x` ATR
- Profit-target candidates: `2.0x` or `3.0x` ATR
- Timeout period: `10` trading days
- Walk-forward training window: `252` trading days
- Walk-forward out-of-sample test window: `63` trading days

Within each walk-forward cycle, the model selects the best parameter set on the prior one-year training window and then applies that choice to the next out-of-sample test block. This keeps the parameter search transparent while reducing simple overfitting.

## Data, Assumptions, and Replication

The data loader follows the same `shinybroker` structure already established in `docs/pull_data.py`: define an IB contract, request historical bars, and convert the result into a pandas DataFrame. The project expects at least two years of daily history, uses next-day-open entries after confirmed signals, assumes `5` basis points of slippage per side, and charges `$1.00` commission per side. If both the stop-loss and the profit target would have been touched on the same bar, the backtest takes the conservative path and assumes the stop-loss happened first.

```{=html}
<p id="project-summary-text"></p>
```

## Performance Metrics

```{=html}
<div id="metrics-container"></div>
```

## Trade Outcome Analysis

The chart below groups every completed trade into one of the required outcome buckets: successful, timed out, or stop-loss triggered.

```{=html}
<div id="outcome-chart"></div>
```

## Trade Ledger and Blotter

Download the complete blotter here: <a id="trade-blotter-link" href="project_outputs/trade_blotter.csv">trade_blotter.csv</a>

```{=html}
<div id="trade-blotter-table"></div>
```

## Equity Curve

```{=html}
<div id="equity-curve-chart"></div>
```

## Walk-Forward Window Log

This table records which parameters were selected in each training window and how many trades appeared in the following out-of-sample block.

```{=html}
<div id="walk-forward-table"></div>
```

```{=html}
<script>
const metricDescriptions = {
  average_return_per_trade_pct: "Average percentage gain or loss generated by each completed trade.",
  sharpe_ratio: "Annualized risk-adjusted return using daily equity changes and the stated risk-free rate.",
  sortino_ratio: "Risk-adjusted return that penalizes downside volatility more than upside swings.",
  max_drawdown_pct: "Largest peak-to-trough decline in the backtest equity curve.",
  win_rate_pct: "Share of trades that finished with a positive dollar PnL.",
  profit_factor: "Gross profits divided by gross losses across all completed trades.",
  expectancy_dollars: "Average dollar PnL per trade after slippage and commissions.",
  final_equity: "Ending account value after compounding all walk-forward trades."
};

function formatMetric(key, value) {
  if (value === null || value === undefined || Number.isNaN(value)) return "N/A";
  if (key.endsWith("_pct") || ["average_return_per_trade_pct", "win_rate_pct", "max_drawdown_pct"].includes(key)) {
    return `${Number(value).toFixed(2)}%`;
  }
  if (["final_equity", "expectancy_dollars"].includes(key)) {
    return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-US", { style: "currency", currency: "USD" }).format(Number(value));
  }
  return Number(value).toFixed(2);
}

function buildMetricsHtml(metrics) {
  const order = [
    "average_return_per_trade_pct",
    "sharpe_ratio",
    "sortino_ratio",
    "max_drawdown_pct",
    "win_rate_pct",
    "profit_factor",
    "expectancy_dollars",
    "final_equity"
  ];
  const cards = order.map((key) => `
    <div class="metric-card">
      <div class="metric-label">${key.replaceAll("_", " ").replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase())}</div>
      <div class="metric-value">${formatMetric(key, metrics[key])}</div>
      <p>${metricDescriptions[key]}</p>
    </div>
  `).join("");
  return `<div class="metric-grid">${cards}</div><p class="metric-footnote">Risk-free rate assumption: ${(Number(metrics.risk_free_rate || 0) * 100).toFixed(2)}%</p>`;
}

function buildHtmlTable(records) {
  if (!records || records.length === 0) {
    return '<div class="placeholder-box"><p>No rows are available for this asset.</p></div>';
  }
  const headers = Object.keys(records[0]);
  const headerHtml = headers.map((header) => `<th>${header}</th>`).join("");
  const rowsHtml = records.map((row) => `<tr>${headers.map((header) => `<td>${row[header] ?? ""}</td>`).join("")}</tr>`).join("");
  return `<table class="table table-striped table-sm"><thead><tr>${headerHtml}</tr></thead><tbody>${rowsHtml}</tbody></table>`;
}

function renderScreeningTable(rows) {
  const container = document.getElementById("screening-table");
  container.innerHTML = buildHtmlTable(rows);
}

function renderAsset(asset, allRows) {
  document.getElementById("active-asset-heading").textContent = asset.symbol;
  document.getElementById("asset-selection-text").textContent =
    `This view shows ${asset.symbol} from the latest screening run. The asset spans ${asset.data_start} to ${asset.data_end}, generated ${asset.metrics.trade_count} trades, and currently has a Sharpe ratio of ${Number(asset.metrics.sharpe_ratio).toFixed(2)}.`;
  document.getElementById("project-summary-text").textContent = asset.project_summary_text;
  document.getElementById("metrics-container").innerHTML = buildMetricsHtml(asset.metrics);
  document.getElementById("trade-blotter-table").innerHTML = buildHtmlTable(asset.trade_blotter_preview);
  document.getElementById("walk-forward-table").innerHTML = buildHtmlTable(asset.walk_forward_windows);
  document.getElementById("trade-blotter-link").href = asset.trade_blotter_csv;

  Plotly.newPlot("outcome-chart", [{
    type: "bar",
    x: ["Successful", "Timed out", "Stop-loss triggered"],
    y: [
      asset.outcome_counts["Successful"] || 0,
      asset.outcome_counts["Timed out"] || 0,
      asset.outcome_counts["Stop-loss triggered"] || 0
    ],
    marker: { color: ["#2a9d8f", "#e9c46a", "#d62828"] }
  }], {
    title: "Trade Outcome Analysis",
    xaxis: { title: "Outcome" },
    yaxis: { title: "Trade count" },
    margin: { l: 20, r: 20, t: 50, b: 20 },
    template: "plotly_white"
  }, { displayModeBar: false, responsive: true });

  Plotly.newPlot("equity-curve-chart", [{
    type: "scatter",
    mode: "lines",
    x: asset.equity_curve.map((row) => row.timestamp),
    y: asset.equity_curve.map((row) => row.equity),
    line: { color: "#0b3d91", width: 3 }
  }], {
    title: "Walk-Forward Equity Curve",
    xaxis: { title: "Date" },
    yaxis: { title: "Equity ($)" },
    margin: { l: 20, r: 20, t: 50, b: 20 },
    template: "plotly_white"
  }, { displayModeBar: false, responsive: true });
}

fetch("project_outputs/asset_dashboard.json")
  .then((response) => response.json())
  .then((dashboard) => {
    const status = document.getElementById("asset-switcher-status");
    if (dashboard.status === "placeholder") {
      status.textContent = dashboard.reason;
      return;
    }

    const select = document.getElementById("asset-select");
    const assetSymbols = Object.keys(dashboard.assets);
    assetSymbols.forEach((symbol) => {
      const option = document.createElement("option");
      option.value = symbol;
      option.textContent = symbol;
      if (symbol === dashboard.selected_symbol) option.selected = true;
      select.appendChild(option);
    });

    renderScreeningTable(dashboard.screening_summary);
    const initialSymbol = dashboard.selected_symbol in dashboard.assets ? dashboard.selected_symbol : assetSymbols[0];
    renderAsset(dashboard.assets[initialSymbol], dashboard.screening_summary);
    status.textContent = `Loaded ${assetSymbols.length} asset views from the latest run.`;

    select.addEventListener("change", (event) => {
      renderAsset(dashboard.assets[event.target.value], dashboard.screening_summary);
      status.textContent = `Showing ${event.target.value}.`;
    });
  })
  .catch((error) => {
    document.getElementById("asset-switcher-status").textContent = `Could not load asset dashboard: ${error}`;
  });
</script>
```
